revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
revision.text: #redirect[[en:September 30 (old lunar calendar)]] <noinclude>[[Category:New automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude><includeonly>[[Category:Erroneous transclusions of September 30 (old lunar calendar)]]</includeonly>

'''旧暦9月30日'''は[[旧暦]][[9月 (旧暦)|9月]]の30日目である。年によっては9月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#友引|友引]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]6年（[[894年]]_月_日） - [[菅原道真]]の建白により[[遣唐使]]の廃止が決定 * [[元禄]]元年（[[1688年]]_月_日） - 元禄に改元 して議会を押える
== 誕生日 == * （[[1472年]]_月_日） - [[王陽明]]、[[明]]の[[儒学者]]・[[陽明学]]の祖
== 忌日 == * [[観応]]2年/[[正平]]6年（[[1351年]]_月_日） - [[夢窓疎石]]、[[臨済宗]]の僧（* [[1275年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
{{msg:旧暦360日}} *前日[[9月29日 (旧暦)|9月29日]] 翌日[[10月1日 (旧暦)|10月1日]] - 前月[[8月30日 (旧暦)|8月30日]] 翌月[[10月30日 (旧暦)|10月30日]] *新暦[[9月30日]]
{{msg:stub}}

{{旧暦9月}} '''旧暦9月30日'''（きゅうれきくがつさんじゅうにち）は、[[9月 (旧暦)|旧暦9月]]の30日目である。年によっては9月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#友引|友引]]である。
== できごと == * [[寛平]]6年（[[ユリウス暦]][[894年]][[11月1日]]） - [[菅原道真]]の建白により[[遣唐使]]の廃止が決定 * [[元禄]]元年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1688年]][[10月23日]]） - [[貞享]]から元禄に[[改元]]
== 誕生日 == * [[成化]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1472年]][[10月31日]]） - [[王陽明]]、[[明]]の[[儒学者]]・[[陽明学]]の祖 * [[1917年]] - [[朴正煕]]、[[軍人]]、[[政治家]]、大韓民国大統領（+ [[1979年]]）
== 忌日 == * [[観応]]2年/[[正平 (日本)|正平]]6年（ユリウス暦[[1351年]][[10月20日]]） - [[夢窓疎石]]、[[臨済宗]]の僧（* [[1275年]]） * [[寛政]]8年（グレゴリオ暦[[1796年]][[10月30日]]） - [[牧野貞長]]、[[政治家]]（* [[1733年]]）
== 記念日・年中行事 ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}
[[Category:japanese lunar calendar]][[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Japanese Lunar Calendar]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]]

{{Lunar September}} '''September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the DAYX day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
==OVERVIEW== {{Lunar September}} ''September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)<!--Lunar September30-->きゅうれきくがつさんじゅうにち）は、[[9月 (旧暦)|Lunar September]]の30日目である。年によっては9月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#友引|友引]][[Category:友引]]である。
== Events == * [[寛平]]6年（[[ユリウス暦]][[894年]][[11月1日]]） - [[菅原道真]]の建白により[[遣唐使]]の廃止が決定 * [[元禄]]元年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1688年]][[10月23日]]） - [[貞享]]から元禄に[[改元]]
== Births == * [[成化]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1472年]][[10月31日]]） - [[王陽明]]、[[明]]の[[儒学者]]・[[陽明学]]の祖 * [[1917年]] - [[朴正煕]]、[[軍人]]、[[政治家]]、大韓民国大統領（+ [[1979年]]）
== Deaths == * [[観応]]2年/[[正平 (日本)|正平]]6年（ユリウス暦[[1351年]][[10月20日]]） - [[夢窓疎石]]、[[臨済宗]]の僧（* [[1275年]]） * [[寛政]]8年（グレゴリオ暦[[1796年]][[10月30日]]） - [[牧野貞長]]、[[政治家]]（* [[1733年]]）
== Commemorations ==
== 関連項目 == {{旧暦360日|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}
[[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Lunar Calendar Days]] [[Category:Lunar September]] {{citation needed}}
{{citation needed}} {{Japanese Traditional Lunar Months}} [[Category:友引]] [[Category:not Butsumetsu]][[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[Category:not Butsumetsu]]

revision.comment: adding info to all the day 30s of the calendar. citation needed see more info
revision.text:
{{Lunar September}} '''September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.{{citation needed}}<!--taken from https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chinese_calendar&oldid=1278636661 this is present on day 30 of all months, so add it to every single page here when you find a source for one of them-->
<!-- {{Lunar September}} ''September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)<!--Lunar September30れきくがつさんじゅうにち）は、[[9月 (旧暦)|Lunar September]]の30日目である。年によっては9月の最終日となる。六曜は[[六曜#友引|友引]][[Category:友引]]である。
--> == Events == * [[寛平]]6年（[[ユリウス暦]][[894年]][[11月1日]]） - [[菅原道真]]の建白により[[遣唐使]]の廃止が決定 * [[元禄]]元年（[[グレゴリオ暦]][[1688年]][[10月23日]]） - [[貞享]]から元禄に[[改元]]
== Births == * [[成化]]8年（ユリウス暦[[1472年]][[10月31日]]） - [[王陽明]]、[[明]]の[[儒学者]]・[[陽明学]]の祖 * [[1917年]] - [[朴正煕]]、[[軍人]]、[[政治家]]、大韓民国大統領（+ [[1979年]]）
== Deaths == * [[観応]]2年/[[正平 (日本)|正平]]6年（ユリウス暦[[1351年]][[10月20日]]） - [[夢窓疎石]]、[[臨済宗]]の僧（* [[1275年]]） * [[寛政]]8年（グレゴリオ暦[[1796年]][[10月30日]]） - [[牧野貞長]]、[[政治家]]（* [[1733年]]）

{{Lunar September}} '''September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the tradional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). revision.text:
[[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}}) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}}) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}}, {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}}.
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}}, {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}}, and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}}) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}}).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}}) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}}).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}}) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}}) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}}, {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}}.
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}}, {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}}, and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}}) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}}).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}}) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}}).

revision.comment: removed [[Category:Untranslated Lunar Calendar Days]]; added [[Category:Lunar calendar translation marker]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
[[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}}) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}}) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}}, {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}}.
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}}, {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}}, and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}}) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}}).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}}) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}}).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also == {{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

[[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]][[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]]{{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}. It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}}.
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}}) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}}.
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|元禄}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}}) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}}) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}}, {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}}.
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}}, {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}}, and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}}).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}}) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}}, a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}}).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|寛政}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}}) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}}, {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}}).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]

[[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] to [[September 30 (Lunisolar)]]: Text replacement - "Old Lunar Calendar" to "Lunisolar"
revision.text:
[[sn:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Old Lunar Calendar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Old Lunar Calendar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Old Lunar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Lunisolar}} . As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).

* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).

revision.text:
[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|遣唐使}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|儒学者}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[Category:Translated pages]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also ==

* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==

* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|軍人}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|観応}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).

There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]

revision.text:
[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472年}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351年}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also! ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894年}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year }} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917年}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979年}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year }} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275年}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733年}} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]] == See Also ==

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year }} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688年}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.

There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year }} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year }} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year }} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year }} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year }} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year }} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year }} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year }} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year }} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year }} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year }} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year }} ).
[[Category:Japanese historical events]] [[Category:Edo period]] [[Category:Meiji period]] [[Category:Confucian scholars]] [[Category:Buddhist monks]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]]
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

[[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year }} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year }} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year }} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year }} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year }} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year }} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year }} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year }} ).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

revision.text:
[[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft}} ).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

revision.text:
[[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

[[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft}} ).

[[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== See Also ==

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages to sort]] to [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:{{subst:PAGENAME}}}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
[[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930}}

[[sn:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ko:음력 9월 30일]] [[ja:9月30日 (旧暦)]] [[zh:九月三十]] [[en:draft:September 30 (Lunisolar)]] [[ja:11月の歴史的出来事]] [[ar:September 30 (Lunisolar)]]

[[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]][[de:September 30 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[cdo:9 nguŏk 30 hô̤ (nùng-lĭk)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]][[de:September 30 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-min-nan:9 goe̍h 30 ji̍t (kū-le̍k)]] [[Category:Pages to sort]] [[Category:qq]][[de:September 30 (Lunisolar)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:September 30 (Lunisolar)}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh-yue:九月三十]] {{Lunisolar September}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|ja|ユリウス暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|ja|グレゴリオ暦|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ).
== See Also ==

revision.text:
{{Lunisolar September|qq=}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September (Nagatsuki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>

* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Lunar Calendar Days" to "Days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar"
revision.text:
{{Lunisolar September|qq=}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September (Nagatsuki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref>Months are also annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
== See Also ==

There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .

== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).

{{afc comment|1=This article as with the other date articles is relatively limited in scope to Japan. Reviewing the translation of this is good and also adding content translated from Chinese and Korean wikipedias will also be good. Both wikipedias are also relatively centered around their own countries for their Lunisolar calendar dates[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 00:17, 28 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{Globalize|article|Japan|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Chinese|1=九月三十|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Expand Korean|1=음력 9월 30일|topic=hist|date=April 2025}} {{Lunisolar September|qq=}} '''September 30''' is the 30th day of month {{ill|September (Lunisolar)|ja|9月 (旧暦)|lt=September (Nagatsuki)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} the 9th month of the traditional {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} . It's {{ill|Rokuyō|ja|六曜|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} day is {{ill|Tomobiki|ja|友引|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
As with all other days 30 of the Sino-Japanese calendar it sometimes happens and sometimes does not depending on the lunar cycle.<ref>https://www.ndl.go.jp/koyomi/e/history/index.html</ref> Months are annotated as either long ({{zh|c=大|l=big|qq=}} for months with 30 days) or short ({{zh|c=小|l=small|qq=}} for months with 29 days). There are also other elements of the traditional Chinese calendar.<ref>https://web.archive.org/web/20250111081847/https://www.chinaknowledge.de/History/Terms/calendar.html</ref><ref>https://www.nippon.com/en/japan-glances/jg00122/the-japanese-calendar.html</ref>
== Events ==
* {{ill|Kanpyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 ({{ill|Julian calendar|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|ja|ユリウス暦|1=Julian calendar|2=ja|3=ユリウス暦|4=zh|5=儒略曆|6=ru|7=Юлианский_календарь|8=de|9=Julianischer_Kalender|10=es|11=Calendario juliano|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|894|ja|894th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|November 1|ja|11月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The abolition of the {{ill|Kentōshi|ja|Kentōshi (envoy to Tang China)|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (Japanese missions to Tang China) is decided based on the recommendation of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|Genroku|ja|Genroku|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 1 ({{ill|Gregorian calendar|1=Gregorian calendar|2=ja|3=グレゴリオ暦|4=fr|5=Calendrier grégorien|6=de|7=Gregorianischer Kalender|8=tl|9=Kalendaryong Gregoryano|10=ru|11=Григорианский календарь|ja|グレゴリオ暦|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|1688|ja|1688th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 23|ja|10月23日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – The era name changes from {{ill|Jōkyō (Japan)|ja|貞享|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} to Genroku.
== Births ==
* {{ill|Chenghua|ja|成化|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Julian {{ill|1472|ja|1472nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 31|ja|10月31日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Wang Yangming|ja|王陽明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|Ming dynasty|ja|明|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|Confucian scholar|ja|1=Ru (Confucian scholar)|儒学者|zh|儒生|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} and founder of {{ill|Yangmingism|ja|陽明学|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} .
* {{ill|1917|ja|1917th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} – {{ill|Park Chung-hee|ja|朴正煕|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|military officer|ja|Military Personnel|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , and President of South Korea (+ {{ill|1979|ja|1979th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
== Deaths ==
* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).

* {{ill|Kan'ō (Japan)|ja|Kan'ō|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 2 / {{ill|Shōhei (Japan)|ja|正平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 6 (Julian {{ill|1351|ja|1351st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 20|ja|10月20日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Musō Soseki|ja|夢窓疎石|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , a monk of the {{ill|Rinzai school|ja|臨済宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1275|ja|1275th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
* {{ill|Kansei|ja|Kansei|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} 8 (Gregorian {{ill|1796|ja|1796年|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} {{ill|October 30|ja|10月30日|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ) – {{ill|Makino Sadachika|ja|牧野貞長|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} , {{ill|politician|ja|政治家|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} (* {{ill|1733|ja|1733rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=}} ).
== See Also ==
{{Lunar Calendar See Also|9|29|10|1|8|30|10|9|0930|qq=}} ==References== {{reflist|qq=}} {{Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}
